title: Delivery worker posts to inbox_uri from DB without re-validating SSRF guard at delivery time
description:
src/delivery.rs process_batch() reads inbox_uri from the delivery_queue table and POSTs directly to it. The SSRF guard (is_private_host) is applied at Follow-accept time in server.rs, but:

1. The inbox_uri is stored in the DB. If the DB is compromised or a row is injected (e.g. via SQL injection elsewhere, or a backup restore), the delivery worker will POST to the stored URI without re-checking it.

2. More practically: DNS rebinding. At Follow time, attacker.com resolves to 1.2.3.4 (public IP, passes is_private_host). Later, TTL expires and DNS resolves to 192.168.1.1 (private). The delivery worker uses the stored URI string 'https://attacker.com/inbox', re-resolves it, and POSTs ActivityPub JSON (signed with the private key) to an internal host.

3. The signed body contains the actor URI and post content, giving an internal service a well-formed HTTPS POST with valid HTTP Signature headers from the broadside private key — potentially useful for confused-deputy attacks.

Mitigation: re-run is_private_host() on the resolved IP at delivery time (requires hooking reqwest's DNS resolution), or at minimum re-validate the URI string before each delivery attempt.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
